MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NATHANIEL M. LEEDS OF CALLAWAY & WOLF, TEL. NO. 541-0300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. REUBEN B. JACOBSON OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6612 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT is Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Brenda L. Crow, CSR #10503, Tel. No. (916)872-7488 =(302/JKS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NATHANIEL M. LEEDS OF CALLAWAY & WOLF, TEL. NO. 541-0300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. REUBEN B. JACOBSON OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6612 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The demurrer to the second cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege specific facts that Defendant acted recklessly and an officer, director, or managing agent engaged, authorized, or ratified the conduct. (Civ. Code sec. 3294(b).) The demurrer to the first cause of action for willful misconduct is sustained without leave to amend. "[T]he need for a separate category of negligence identified as willful misconduct, which was designed to alleviate the inequity of the all-or-nothing contributory negligence rule, has disappeared with the adoption of a rule of comparative negligence." (Southern Pacific Transportation Co. v. The State of California (1981) 115 Cal.App.3d 116, 121.) The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligence per se is sustained without leave to amend. Negligence per se is an evidentiary doctrine, not a cause of action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Brenda L. Crow, CSR #10503, Tel. No. (916)872-7488 =(302/JKS)
